episodes.title: Recovery from Jehovah's Witnesses: Session 1 - You Are Not Broken
episodes.description:
Psychologist, cult-deprogrammer and ex-Jehovah's Witness explains how to create a new authentic identity after leaving a high control group.
Download the handout for this session: https://session1-rebuildingyouridentity.tiiny.site/1.png
Here’s a YouTube description designed to:

* build emotional connection
* establish credibility
* improve discoverability/search
* encourage engagement
* softly funnel viewers toward Patreon/community

Leaving Jehovah’s Witnesses can feel like losing your entire world.

Not just your religion —
but your identity, certainty, relationships, future plans, and sense of self.

In this first recovery lesson, we explore why so many former Jehovah’s Witnesses feel lost after leaving, and why that reaction is completely understandable.

We’ll talk about:
• identity reconstruction
• conditioning and fear
• grief and emotional confusion
• rebuilding self-esteem
• challenging inherited beliefs
• the pressure to become an “exJW stereotype”
• discovering who YOU want to become

One of the most important ideas in this video is this:

You do not “find yourself.”
You create yourself.

If you’ve recently left the organisation, are questioning your beliefs, or are rebuilding your life after a high-control environment, I hope this helps you feel less alone.

Healing is not about becoming who you were before.
It is about consciously building who you choose to become now.

📌 Topics Covered:
• Jehovah’s Witness recovery
• Religious trauma
• Identity after leaving a cult
• Rebuilding self-esteem
• Psychological effects of shunning
• Fear, guilt & conditioning
• Life after Jehovah’s Witnesses
• Personal growth & healing

💬 If this resonated with you, feel free to share your experience in the comments. Many people reading may feel exactly the same way you once did.

👍 Like & Subscribe for more videos on:
• psychology
• recovery from high-control groups
• identity rebuilding
• emotional healing
• confidence & self-development

#exjw #jehovahswitnesses #religioustrauma #cultrecovery #psychology #mentalhealth #healing #selfesteem #identity #exjehovahswitness

episodes.title: Overcoming The Fear Of Armageddon - Ex-Jehovah's Witness Psychologist Explains
episodes.description:
ex-jwrecovery.com

Many former Jehovah’s Witnesses carry a lingering fear: “What if Armageddon really is coming?”

When world events escalate — wars, pandemics, political tension — that old conditioning can resurface. Some former members even feel tempted to return to the organisation out of fear, not belief.

In this video, I explore why this fear happens and, more importantly, how to overcome it.

As a psychologist and a former Jehovah’s Witness myself, I explain the psychological mechanisms behind Armageddon anxiety and share practical strategies you can use to calm your mind, regain perspective, and reclaim your sense of safety in the world.

This video will help you:

• Understand why apocalyptic fear can persist long after leaving
• Recognise the psychological conditioning behind Armageddon beliefs
• Learn how high-control groups use fear to maintain loyalty
• Develop practical tools to manage anxiety when world events feel triggering
• Rebuild confidence in your own judgement and worldview

Leaving a high-control religion is not just about changing beliefs — it’s about rewiring emotional responses that were conditioned over years or decades.

If you’ve ever felt a sudden wave of fear when the news talks about war, “peace and security,” or global crisis, you’re not alone. This reaction is extremely common among former members, and it can be overcome.

The goal is not just to stop fearing Armageddon — it’s to help you live freely without that psychological shadow over your life.

Psychologist Explains - Being a Jehovah’s Witness Affects Your Mental Health
episodes.description:
In this video, I explore how life as one of Jehovah’s Witnesses can quietly erode mental health over time.
Using the Biopsychosocial Model, I break down how the JW lifestyle affects us biologically, psychologically, and socially — from constant anxiety about Armageddon, to low self-esteem, guilt, and chronic loneliness.

Growing up in a fear-based, high-control environment changes how you think, feel, and even how your body reacts to stress. Many former Witnesses leave feeling depressed, anxious, and unsure of who they are — but it’s not because you’re broken. It’s because you were conditioned to survive in an unhealthy system.

episodes.title: Psychologist Critiques Jehovah's Witness Elder's Training Video on Suicide
episodes.description:
In this video, I critique a Jehovah’s Witness elders’ training video that attempts to address grief and suicidal ideation. As a psychologist and former Jehovah’s Witness, I bring a unique lens to this discussion—one informed by both lived experience and psychological training.

This so-called "training" exposes deep flaws in how the religion handles vulnerable people in crisis. Instead of encouraging emotional expression, access to professional help, or healthy grieving, it reinforces guilt, moralizes suicidal thoughts, and pushes adherence to doctrine over actual care.

Some of the key issues I address include:

    The moral framing of suicidal ideation as comparable to "immoral thoughts"

    The absence of any recommendation to seek medical or psychological support

    The use of resurrection hope as an emotional anchor that delays healing

    The dangerous use of scripture and "integrity" as solutions to mental distress

    The lack of follow-up or meaningful aftercare

This is more than a critique—it's a call to raise awareness about how religious ideology can interfere with proper mental health support, especially for those dealing with loss.

If you’re an ex-JW, a mental health professional, or someone trying to understand the intersection between high-control religion and emotional trauma, this video is for you.

👉 Subscribe for more psychologically-informed analysis of Jehovah’s Witness teachings and support for those transitioning out of the religion.

episodes.title: What Jehovah's Witnesses Actually Believe - Their Secret End Of The World Prophecy
episodes.description: From the Annual General Meeting of Jehovah's Witnesses - where only a few of the most trusted members may attend - their governing body leaders reveal their prophecy of the end of the world.

episodes.title: 10 Steps For Recovering From Jehovah's Witnesses
episodes.description: Here, I lay out my 10 steps for recovering from Jehovah's Witness indoctrination.

episodes.title: Jehovah's Witnesses Crazy "Overlapping Generation" Teaching
episodes.description: In 1984, Jehovahs Witnesses claimed that those alive in 1914 "will never die" since they were the generation that Jesus was talking about in Matthew 24:34. Since their 1914 prophecy has failed, here is Governing Body member David Splane trying to claim that when Jesus said "this generation" he really meant TWO overlapping generations.

episodes.title: Jehovah's Witnesses 143rd Gilead Graduation CRINGEWORTHY Song
episodes.description: Just when you think they couldn't get more culty, they bring out this shit. Quite possibly the most cringeworthy thing I've ever seen the Watchtower release since my exit of this cult.

episodes.title: Judge Rutherford - Armageddon Survivors
episodes.description: Taken from the record by Judge Rutherford, former leader of the Jehovah's Witnesses from 1917 - 1942.

episodes.title: Derren Brown Explains How Limiting Our Own Perspectives Can Be
episodes.description: Take from Derren Browns "The System" - this little speech is a great explanation of how we can be blinded by outside information by only looking at things through OUR OWN perspective.

episodes.title: Jehovahs Witnesses Applaud 10yo Girl For Shunning Her Own Sister
episodes.description: Video secretly recorded in the 2017 convention entitled "Don't Give Up!". Here a 10yo girl has been indoctrinated into shunning her own sister who left the cult. She is given a round of applause.

episodes.title: Jehovahs Witness Governing Body - Anthony Morris III Claims To Be Jesus Brother
episodes.description: Anthony Morris III claims Jesus is his "big brother" and that all those not going to heaven have the "honor" of serving Christs brothers (him) from heaven. Can you say CULT?

episodes.title: The Governing Body Of Jehovah's Witnesses
episodes.description: In this video I take a look at the evidence for and against the Governing Body being Gods sole channel of communication to mankind.

episodes.title: Interview With Ex JW Howie Tran - Assistant To The Govering Body
episodes.description:
Listen to the absolutely fascinating interview with Howie Rutledge Tran, who served as a Personal Assistant to several members of the Governing Body at Brooklyn Bethel for 11 years.
As well as personal insights about the behaviour and perculiarities of members of the Governing Body and the Bethel family, Howie relates how 'new light' was created.
Howie also relates his own heartbreaking personal journey, and how he fought for over a decade to deny who he was, before finding freedom, at a great cost.
If you don't listen to any other JWPodcast, listen to this one.

episodes.title: Hilarious Stephen Lett Talk Intro
episodes.description: I tried so hard not to laugh but this is just too hilarious. Governing Body memeber Stephen Lett talks about singing praises to Jehovah in his unique style.

episodes.title: Deborah Frances-White - Cult Following
episodes.description: Ex-Jehovahs Witness Deborah Frances-White delivery a very accurate and very funny account of her younger years as a Jehovah's Witness in Australia.

episodes.title: Deborah Frances-White - Saving Brother Ryan
episodes.description: Ex-Jehovah's Witness Deborah Frances-White delivers a very accurate and very funny standup of helping another witness leave the cult.

episodes.title: 7 Things Most Jehovah's Witnesses Don't Know They're Supposed To Believe
episodes.description: From Watchtowers own publications, here's seven things most witnesses don't know they're supposed to believe.

episodes.title: HIDDEN RECORDING: Disfellowshipped Girl Begs To Be Reinstated As A Jehovah's Witness
episodes.description: This was recorded secretly by a girl hoping to see her family again. She begs and cries at the Jehovah's Witness elders hardheartedness because they believe she may have had contact with her family while disfellowshipped. It's a heartbreaking ending.

episodes.title: Leaked Jehovah's Witness Talk - Depression Is A Part Of This Life
episodes.description: Brother Nathan Fuel delivers a talk entitled Depression Is A Part Of This Life. This is one of many talks i was ordered NOT to share around. It's worth noting that many many Jehovah's Witnesses suffer from depression, anxiety and guilt mainly due to them being told they're not good enough, not doing enough and no matter how well they're doing it's never 100% certain that they'll survive armageddon (which is just around the corner, as of 1890).

episodes.title: Leaked Jehovah's Witness Talk - Beware The Spirit Of Independence
episodes.description: This talk was specifically asked NOT to be shared around. So here it is. Judge for yourself wether you think this is a cult or not. Brother Melvin Mallett delivers a talk entitled "Beware of independence".

episodes.title: J F Rutherford - Religion Is A Snare And A Racket
episodes.description: One of Watchtower leader J F Rutherfords most famous door to door recorded sermons. Jehovah's Witnesses in the 1930s would go door to door and play one of his audio recordings to all who would listen.
